methods.description: Extraction of total genomic DNA from dried plant tissue using the DNeasy 96 plant mini kit.

steps.description: Total genomic DNA was extracted from similar amounts of dried tissue (c. 10 mg) with the DNeasy 96 plant mini kit (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany) following the manufacturer's protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA from plant tissue using DNeasy 96 kit

methods.description: Amplified Fragment Length Polymorphism (AFLP) procedure for genetic fingerprinting, including primer screening, PCR, and error rate calculation.

steps.description: The AFLP procedure followed Vos et al. (1995) with the modifications described in Schö nswetter et al. (2009). Initially, selective primers were screened using 16 primer combinations.
steps.searchable_summary: screen selective primers for AFLP using 16 combinations

steps.description: The three final primer combinations for the selective PCR (fluorescent dye in brackets) were EcoRI (6-Fam)-ACA ⁄ MseI-CAT, EcoRI (VIC)-AGG ⁄ MseI-CTC, and EcoRI (NED)-ACA ⁄ MseI-CAC.
steps.searchable_summary: select final primer combinations for AFLP PCR

steps.description: Purification and visualization of PCR products as well as scoring were carried out as described in Rebernig et al. (2010b).
steps.searchable_summary: purify and visualize AFLP PCR products

steps.description: Six plants were extracted twice to test the reproducibility of AFLP fingerprinting (Bonin et al. 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: test reproducibility of AFLP fingerprinting

steps.description: Seven samples were used as replicates between PCR plates and were replicated more than twice, resulting in a total of 75 replicates. The error rate (Bonin et al. 2004) was calculated as the ratio of mismatches (scoring of 0 vs. 1) over phenotypic comparisons in AFLP profiles of replicated individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate error rate for AFLP using replicates

methods.description: PCR amplification of nrDNA ITS region and plastid spacers, including cloning for samples with length polymorphism.

steps.description: PCR products of the nrDNA ITS region and the plastid spacers trnL-rpl32, rps16-trnK, psbD-trnT and petL-psbE were obtained in reaction volumes of 13 lL including 4.5 lL REDTaq ReadyMix PCR mix (Sigma-Aldrich, Steinheim, Germany), 0.5 lL of 1 mg ⁄ mL bovine serum albumin (BSA; Promega, Madison, WI, USA), 0.5 lL 10 lM each of forward and reverse primers and about 25 ng of DNA suspended in 1 lL 1· TAE buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR for nrDNA ITS and plastid spacers

steps.description: For ITS, primers and PCR conditions were as in Blö ch et al. (2009). Plastid spacers were amplified using the primers of Shaw et al. (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: use specific primers and conditions for ITS and plastid spacers

steps.description: Some samples from the outgroup taxa repeatedly failed to amplify and were amplified using newly designed internal primers listed in Table S3.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify outgroup taxa samples with new internal primers

steps.description: PCR conditions were as follows: 95°C for 1 min followed by 10 cycles each consisting of 30 s at 95°C, 30 s at 47°C, 1.5 min at 65°C; 20 cycles each of 30 s at 95°C, 30 s at 49°C, 1.5 min at 65°C; and a final elongation step of 8 min at 65°C.
steps.searchable_summary: set PCR conditions for amplification

steps.description: ITS PCR products from individuals in which length polymorphism made direct sequencing impossible were cloned using the pGEM-T-easy vector system and JM109 competent cells (Promega) following the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: clone ITS PCR products with length polymorphism

steps.description: Inserts of 8-15 clones per individual were amplified using universal primers M13F(-47) and M13R(-48) and 1 lL of colony resuspended in 70 lL 1· TBE.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify inserts of clones using M13 primers

steps.description: PCR conditions were the same as described above except for annealing and elongation temperatures of 62 and 72°C, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust PCR conditions for clone amplification

steps.description: PCR products were purified with E. coli Exonuclease I and FastAP Thermosensitive Alkaline Phosphatase (Fermentas, St. Leon-Rot, Germany) following the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: purify PCR products with Exonuclease I and FastAP

steps.description: Cycle sequencing reactions were performed using 5 lL of purified template and 1 lL BigDye Terminator (PE Applied Biosystems), then cleaned with Sephadex G-50 Fine (GE Healthcare Bio-Sciences, Uppsala, Sweden) and sequenced on an ABI 3770 DNA Analyzer (PE Applied Biosystems).
steps.searchable_summary: perform cycle sequencing and clean with Sephadex

methods.description: Analysis of AFLP data to infer population structure and genetic covariance using Bayesian clustering and principal coordinate analysis.

steps.description: All monomorphic fragments and the ones present in all but one individual of the diploid data set were removed from the data set to avoid biased parameter estimates (Bonin et al. 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: remove monomorphic fragments from AFLP data

steps.description: Population structure was inferred employing a Bayesian clustering approach developed for dominant markers (STRUCTURE 2.2; Pritchard et al. 2000;Falush et al. 2007) with an admixture model with uncorrelated allele frequencies and recessive alleles.
steps.searchable_summary: infer population structure using Bayesian clustering

steps.description: Ten replicate runs for each K (number of groups) ranging from 1 to 10 were carried out at the Bioportal of the University of Oslo (http://www.bioportal.uio.no/), using a burn-in of 10 5 iterations followed by 10 6 additional MCMC iterations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform replicate runs for Bayesian clustering

steps.description: Similarity among results of different runs for the same K was calculated according to Nordborg et al. (2005) using STRUCTURE-SUM-2009 (Ehrich 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate similarity among Bayesian clustering runs

steps.description: We identified the optimal number of groups as the value of K where the likelihood reached a plateau; the results of replicate runs were identical, and no empty groups were encountered.
steps.searchable_summary: identify optimal number of groups for population structure

steps.description: Replicate runs of the best K were merged with CLUMPP 1.1.1 (Jakobsson & Rosenberg 2007) using the 'Full Search'-algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: merge replicate runs using CLUMPP

steps.description: The relative 'cluster membership coefficients' of all individuals were then averaged for each population.
steps.searchable_summary: average cluster membership coefficients

steps.description: A principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) based on a matrix of Nei72 distances (Nei 1972) among individuals was calculated using the modules 'Simgend', 'Dcenter' and 'Eigen' from NTSYS-PC 2.2 (Rohlf 1997).
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCoA using Nei72 distances

methods.description: Analysis of sequence data including assembly, alignment, haplotype diversity estimation, and phylogeographic analyses.

steps.description: Sequences were assembled using SEQMAN II (DNAStar, Madison, WI, USA), manually edited and aligned with BIOEDIT 7.0.5.2 (Hall 1999).
steps.searchable_summary: assemble and align sequences

steps.description: Within-population haplotype diversity was estimated using p, the mean number of pairwise differences (Tajima 1983), calculated with ARLEQUIN 3.11 (Excoffier et al. 2005).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate haplotype diversity using ARLEQUIN

steps.description: Nuclear ITS and plastid sequence data were analysed using statistical parsimony as implemented in TCS (Clement et al. 2000) with the connection limit set to 95%.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical parsimony analysis using TCS

steps.description: Phylogeographic analyses of the plastid data set were conducted in BEAST 1.6 (Drummond & Rambaut 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct phylogeographic analysis using BEAST
